Self-Directed Brokerage Accounts

Take control of the money in your 401(k).

If your employer plan offers an SDBA window, you can move beyond the default fund menu into a professionally managed portfolio — without leaving your plan.

Retire in Idaho

Your Idaho Retirement

Deserves a Real Plan.

Idaho is one of the best states to retire in — no Social Security tax, low cost of living, and incredible quality of life. But without the right plan, you could leave thousands on the table. We help Idaho retirees optimize every dollar.

Get Your Free Retirement Assessment

"Your retirement income strategy should be as unique as your situation."

Mike Stevens, Founder
Why Idaho

Idaho's Retirement Tax Advantages

Social Security Is Tax-Free

Idaho doesn't tax Social Security benefits — period. That's money in your pocket that many other states take.

Retirement Income Deduction

Qualifying pension and retirement plan income is eligible for deduction, reducing your Idaho taxable income.

No Estate or Inheritance Tax

Idaho won't take a cut when you pass wealth to your family. No state estate tax, no inheritance tax.

Your Savings Go Further

Idaho's cost of living is significantly lower than California, Oregon, and Washington — the states most people relocate from.

What We Do

Retirement Planning for Idaho Families

Retirement Income Planning

Turn your 401(k), IRA, pension, and Social Security into a reliable monthly income stream — optimized for Idaho taxes.

Tax-Efficient Withdrawals

The order you pull from accounts matters. We sequence withdrawals to keep you in the lowest possible tax bracket.

Federal Employee Benefits

FERS, TSP, FEHB — Idaho has a large federal workforce (INL, VA, USDA, Forest Service). We know these benefits inside out.

Social Security Strategy

When you claim matters more than most people think. We model the optimal filing strategy for your situation.

401(k) & IRA Rollovers

Leaving a job or consolidating accounts? We guide the rollover to avoid tax surprises and set up better investments.

Estate & Legacy Planning

Idaho's lack of estate tax is an advantage — but you still need a plan. We help protect and transfer wealth efficiently.

How It Works

Three Steps to Clarity

We serve Idaho families through secure virtual meetings. Same comprehensive planning — no drive to Utah required.

1

Free Consultation

30 minutes with Mike Stevens. No cost, no sales pitch. Just an honest look at where you stand.

2

Full Analysis

We model your complete financial picture — income, taxes, Social Security, benefits, risk.

3

Your Money Map

A personalized Retirement Money Map showing exactly how to make your savings last.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Idaho a good state to retire in? +

Idaho is one of the most tax-friendly states for retirees. Social Security benefits are not taxed, there's a retirement income deduction for qualifying pensions, no estate or inheritance tax, and the cost of living is lower than most western states. Combined with outdoor recreation and strong communities, Idaho consistently ranks as a top retirement destination.

Does Idaho tax Social Security or retirement income? +

Idaho does not tax Social Security benefits. For other retirement income like pensions and 401(k) withdrawals, Idaho allows a deduction for qualifying retirement benefits. We help clients structure withdrawals to minimize their overall Idaho tax burden.

Can I work with Capital Wealth if I live in Idaho? +

Yes. We serve Idaho clients through secure virtual meetings. You get the same comprehensive planning experience — screen sharing, document review, and personalized strategy — from the comfort of your home. Many of our Idaho clients prefer this to driving to an office.

What should I do with my 401(k) when I retire in Idaho? +

It depends on your full financial picture. Rolling over to an IRA can give you more investment options and flexibility, but timing and tax implications matter. We analyze your specific situation to determine the best rollover strategy for Idaho's tax environment.

How much do I need to retire in Idaho? +

There's no one-size-fits-all number, but Idaho's lower cost of living means your savings stretch further than in states like California, Oregon, or Washington. We build a personalized Retirement Money Map that shows exactly how much income you can generate and how long it will last.

Do you help with federal employee retirement in Idaho? +

Yes. We specialize in FERS retirement planning, TSP optimization, and FEHB decisions. Idaho has a significant federal workforce — INL, VA, USDA, Forest Service — and we understand the unique benefits and decisions federal employees face at retirement.

Ready for a retirement plan that actually works?

No pressure. No sales pitch. Just a clear-eyed look at your numbers with a fiduciary who's legally obligated to put your interests first.

Schedule Your Free Assessment
